Title: Discovery of GDSL-domain proteins as key players for suberin polymerization and degradation

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GDSL esterase/lipase family; SGNH hydrolase-type lipase
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Suberin polymerization in endodermal cell wall
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Promotes apoplastic polymerization/assembly of suberin monomers into suberin lamellae
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Apoplastic diffusion barrier for nutrient and water transport; contributes to salt tolerance and elemental homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in endodermis and epidermis; repressed by prolonged auxin; induced by ABA and CIF2
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in endodermis and epidermis; repressed by prolonged auxin; induced by ABA and CIF2
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Auxin (NAA) represses expression; ABA and CIF2 induce expression; mild salt stress reveals barrier defect phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 quintuple knockout analysis; Fluorol Yellow and Nile Red staining; TEM; chemical depolymerization/GC-MS of suberin monomers; FDA penetration assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for normal root suberin deposition; loss in quintuple mutant abolishes detectable endodermal suberin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GELP22 belongs to an auxin-repressed set of five GELPs associated with endodermal suberization. 2. Single mutants showed no clear phenotype, but combined quintuple mutants lacking GELP22/38/49/51/96 lost nearly all root suberin. 3. The data support a direct role in suberin polymerization rather than indirect transcriptional regulation.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for suberin polymerization established genetically at the redundant module level
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Quintuple loss causes hypersensitivity to mild salt stress and defective apoplastic barrier, but no obvious Casparian strip defect
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for engineering root suberin barrier strength and nutrient homeostasis; likely requires multiplex editing or stacking because of redundancy
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, GEO: GSE153478; chemical suberin profiling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function is supported at the five-gene module level; individual catalytic activity of GELP22 was not biochemically demonstrated in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter reporters; ELTPproXVE for inducible endodermal expression platform used in study

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GDSL esterase/lipase family; SGNH hydrolase-type lipase
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Suberin polymerization in endodermal cell wall
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Promotes apoplastic polymerization/assembly of suberin monomers into suberin lamellae
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Apoplastic diffusion barrier for nutrient and water transport; contributes to salt tolerance and elemental homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Endodermis-specific; repressed by prolonged auxin; induced by ABA and CIF2 and expanded into cortex
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Endodermis-specific; repressed by prolonged auxin; induced by ABA and CIF2 and expanded into cortex
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Auxin (NAA) represses expression; ABA and CIF2 induce expression; mild salt stress reveals barrier defect phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 single and quintuple mutants; inducible complementation with GELP38proXVE>>GELP38:mCitrine; Fluorol Yellow and Nile Red staining; TEM; chemical depolymerization/GC-MS; FDA penetration assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required as part of the redundant GELP22/38/49/51/96 module for root suberin deposition; complementation restores suberin lamellae
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GELP38 expression strongly correlates with endodermal suberization and is induced by ABA/CIF2 but repressed by auxin. 2. Quintuple mutants lacking GELP38 together with four related GELPs completely lack detectable endodermal suberin. 3. GELP38-mCITRINE localizes to the apoplast, and inducible expression in the quintuple background restores suberin, strongly supporting a direct polymerization role.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for suberin polymerization established genetically at the redundant module level
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Quintuple loss causes hypersensitivity to mild salt stress and defective apoplastic barrier, but no obvious Casparian strip defect
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for boosting root suberization, but redundancy suggests stacked manipulation with related GELPs is preferable
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, GEO: GSE153478; chemical suberin profiling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is the most directly supported member because localization and complementation were shown specifically for GELP38

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GDSL esterase/lipase family; SGNH hydrolase-type lipase
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Suberin polymerization in endodermal cell wall
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Promotes apoplastic polymerization/assembly of suberin monomers into suberin lamellae
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Apoplastic diffusion barrier for nutrient and water transport; contributes to salt tolerance and elemental homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in endodermis and epidermis; repressed by prolonged auxin; induced by ABA and CIF2
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in endodermis and epidermis; repressed by prolonged auxin; induced by ABA and CIF2
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Auxin (NAA) represses expression; ABA and CIF2 induce expression; mild salt stress reveals barrier defect phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA single mutant plus CRISPR/Cas9 quintuple knockout analysis; Fluorol Yellow and Nile Red staining; TEM; chemical depolymerization/GC-MS; FDA penetration assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required as part of the redundant GELP22/38/49/51/96 module for normal root suberin deposition
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GELP49 is part of the auxin-repressed GELP set tightly associated with suberin deposition. 2. Single mutant lacks strong phenotype, indicating redundancy. 3. Combined quintuple loss with related GELPs causes near-complete elimination of root suberin.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for suberin polymerization established genetically at the redundant module level
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Quintuple loss causes hypersensitivity to mild salt stress and defective apoplastic barrier
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Multiplex manipulation may help engineer stronger suberin barriers; single-gene targeting may be insufficient
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, GEO: GSE153478; chemical suberin profiling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct localization or individual enzymatic assay shown for GELP49

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GDSL esterase/lipase family; SGNH hydrolase-type lipase
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Suberin depolymerization during lateral root emergence
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Can degrade/remodel endodermal suberin when ectopically induced in the endodermis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Root endodermal cell walls overlying lateral root primordia
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Barrier controlling apoplastic transport; remodeling required for lateral root emergence
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Auxin-induced; induced in endodermal cells overlying lateral root primordia from stage I to IV; low cortex or no signal without lateral root formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Auxin-induced; induced in endodermal cells overlying lateral root primordia from stage I to IV; low cortex or no signal without lateral root formation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Auxin (NAA) induces expression; estradiol-inducible endodermal overexpression triggers suberin loss during lateral root context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter reporters; inducible endodermis-specific overexpression (ELTPproXVE); Fluorol Yellow staining; transfer assay; TEM
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression in endodermis causes disappearance of suberin staining, supporting degradative capacity
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Suberin degradation may weaken barrier properties; single mutant had no major detectable developmental delay in assays reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to modulate root barrier plasticity and facilitate controlled remodeling, but likely redundant and context dependent
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper supports degradative capacity upon overexpression, but necessity in vivo remains unresolved
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: ELTPproXVE inducible endodermal promoter; native promoter reporters

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GDSL esterase/lipase family; SGNH hydrolase-type lipase
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Suberin depolymerization during lateral root emergence
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Can degrade/remodel endodermal suberin when ectopically induced in the endodermis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Root endodermal cell walls overlying lateral root primordia
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Barrier controlling apoplastic transport; remodeling required for lateral root emergence
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Auxin-induced; induced in endodermal cells overlying lateral root primordia from stage I to IV; low cortex or no signal without lateral root formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Auxin-induced; induced in endodermal cells overlying lateral root primordia from stage I to IV; low cortex or no signal without lateral root formation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Auxin (NAA) induces expression; estradiol-inducible endodermal overexpression triggers suberin loss
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter reporters; inducible endodermis-specific overexpression (ELTPproXVE); Fluorol Yellow staining
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression in endodermis causes disappearance of suberin staining, supporting degradative capacity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GELP55 is auxin induced in the endodermis during lateral root emergence. 2. Endodermis-specific inducible overexpression removes suberin staining. 3. A clear loss-of-function requirement for suberin degradation was not established in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential weakening of barrier if overactivated
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible lever for tuning root barrier remodeling, but use requires caution because necessity and specificity are not fully established
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Single mutant was reported with delayed lateral root development only for gelp72, not gelp55
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: ELTPproXVE inducible endodermal promoter; native promoter reporters

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GDSL esterase/lipase family; SGNH hydrolase-type lipase
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Suberin depolymerization during lateral root emergence
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Can degrade/remodel endodermal suberin when induced in the endodermis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Root endodermal cell walls overlying lateral root primordia
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Barrier controlling apoplastic transport; remodeling required for lateral root emergence
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Auxin-induced; induced in endodermal cells overlying lateral root primordium from stage III onward and also in outer layer of growing primordium
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Auxin-induced; induced in endodermal cells overlying lateral root primordium from stage III onward and also in outer layer of growing primordium
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Auxin (NAA) induces expression; estradiol-inducible endodermal overexpression triggers suberin loss
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter reporters; inducible endodermis-specific overexpression (ELTPproXVE); Fluorol Yellow staining; loss-of-function mutant analysis in gravistimulation assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression causes suberin disappearance; single mutants display delayed lateral root development, supporting a role in barrier remodeling during emergence
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GELP72 is auxin induced in endodermal cells around lateral root primordia and in the primordium outer layer. 2. Endodermal overexpression removes suberin staining. 3. Unlike other degradase candidates tested, gelp72 single mutants showed delayed lateral root development in both alleles and both time points, supporting an in vivo role.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced degradation may compromise barrier function; loss delays lateral root emergence
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for tuning lateral root emergence versus barrier integrity tradeoff
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Among the overexpression-positive degradases, GELP72 had the strongest loss-of-function support
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: ELTPproXVE inducible endodermal promoter; native promoter reporters

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GDSL esterase/lipase family; SGNH hydrolase-type lipase
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Suberin remodeling during lateral root emergence
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Candidate suberin-remodeling GDSL enzyme; overexpression did not clearly remove suberin staining in this study
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Root endodermal cell walls overlying lateral root primordia
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Barrier controlling apoplastic transport; remodeling implicated in lateral root emergence
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Auxin-responsive; expressed in endodermis prior to lateral root initiation and induced in overlying endodermal cells from stage I to IV
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Auxin-responsive; expressed in endodermis prior to lateral root initiation and induced in overlying endodermal cells from stage I to IV
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter reporters; inducible endodermis-specific overexpression; loss-of-function mutant analysis in gravistimulation assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Single mutants display delayed lateral root emergence, but overexpression did not clearly trigger suberin loss; role in suberin degradation remains candidate-level
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GELP73 is induced in endodermal cells associated with lateral root emergence. 2. Endodermal overexpression did not clearly remove suberin staining. 3. Nevertheless, single mutants displayed delayed lateral root emergence, indicating relevance to the process but not proving degradase activity.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May affect lateral root emergence if perturbed
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for tuning root developmental barrier remodeling, but mechanism needs further validation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core candidate because it was a main experimentally tested auxin-induced GELP, though direct degradase function was not demonstrated
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: ELTPproXVE inducible endodermal promoter; native promoter reporters

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GDSL esterase/lipase family; SGNH hydrolase-type lipase
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Suberin remodeling during lateral root emergence
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Candidate suberin-remodeling GDSL enzyme; overexpression did not clearly remove suberin staining in this study
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Root endodermal cell walls overlying lateral root primordia
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Barrier controlling apoplastic transport; remodeling implicated in lateral root emergence
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Auxin-responsive; expressed in endodermis prior to lateral root initiation, induced in overlying endodermal cells from stage I to IV, and from stage IV in outer layer of primordium
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Auxin-responsive; expressed in endodermis prior to lateral root initiation, induced in overlying endodermal cells from stage I to IV, and from stage IV in outer layer of primordium
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter reporters; inducible endodermis-specific overexpression; loss-of-function mutant analysis in gravistimulation assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Single mutants display delayed lateral root emergence, but overexpression did not clearly trigger suberin loss; role in suberin degradation remains candidate-level
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GELP81 is expressed in endodermal cells and later in the primordium outer layer during lateral root emergence. 2. Endodermal overexpression did not clearly remove suberin staining. 3. Single mutants delayed lateral root emergence, implicating the gene in this remodeling program.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May affect lateral root emergence if perturbed
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for modulating root developmental remodeling, but its biochemical role remains unresolved
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core experimentally tested candidate, though direct degradase activity was not shown
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: ELTPproXVE inducible endodermal promoter; native promoter reporters
